Atomic vector of a given builtin type

Source: R/type_spec.R
t_vector_of.Rd

Like t_list_of() but for atomic vectors. The element type must be a builtin scalar type name ("numeric", "integer", "character", ...).

Usage

t_vector_of(type, min_length = 0, max_length = Inf, exact_length = NULL)

Arguments

type

Builtin element type name.

min_length

Minimum length.

max_length

Maximum length (defaults to Inf).

exact_length

If non-NULL, vector must have exactly this length.

Value

A type_spec of kind "vector_of".

See also

Other type specifications: is_type_spec(), t_enum(), t_list_of(), t_model(), t_nullable(), t_predicate(), t_union(), type_spec

Examples

triple <- t_vector_of("integer", exact_length = 3L)
is_type(1:3, triple)
#> [1] TRUE
is_type(1:5, triple)
#> [1] FALSE
